utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi alan , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi , good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good to see you as well . are you ready to get started ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , alan is a 61-year-old male , here with complaints of shortness of breath .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so alan , what's going on ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so over the weekend , uh , i took a very long bike ride , about three hours or so and i got back , i was very light headed , i was short of breath and it really surprised me because i pretty much bike ride every morning and it was never an issue before so i'm a little concerned about that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and have you had any other symptoms ? have you had any fever-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: n-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . chest pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , but i felt very light headed and , uh , i- it was hard to catch my breath afterwards , so maybe i did have a little chest pain but , uh , it , it eventually went away after about an hour .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , um , and anyone else sick in your , in your family ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and any cough ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , i had a little bit of a cough with my first cold in the last year and a half , but that went away after about a week or so .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . um , and any other symptoms ? i know you went through the review of symptoms-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . with the nurse but any nausea or vomiting or abdominal pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: n- no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , i know that i've treated you for migraines in the past . how are you doing with those ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's been under control . i've , uh , taken medication , i've , uh , avoided bright lights and it's so far so good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and i think we had you on imitrex for that . have you-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i'm still taking that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . and then your reflux , how are you doing with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: managing that as well .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and everything's working out well with the protonix ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and then i know you had the cataract surgery about three months ago , your vision is okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that was about four and a half months ago and it's been actually great ever since .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . all right , well , we're gon na go ahead and do a quick physical exam . i wan na see your vital signs first .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , show me the vital signs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so looking here , everything looks quite good . your oxygenation is perfect , which is wonderful . let me just take a listen .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: deep breath . another one . good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , on your physical exam , your neck is nice and supple . i do n't appreciate any jugular venous distension . your heart is in a regular rhythm , i do n't hear any murmur . your lungs do have a slight wheeze , expiratory wheeze , bilaterally . i can hear it every time you breathe and i see trace lower extremity edema in your lower extremities .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so what does all that mean ? you know , you , uh , i'm not quite sure yet but we're gon na review some of your results . hey dragon , show me the chest x-ray .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so looking here , this is a very normal chest x-ray . um , i do n't see any pulmonary edema , there's no pneumonia , so we're viewing your chest x-ray as , it's a norm- it's normal .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , let's look at your pulmonary function test .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , show me your , the pfts . and looking here again , these look quite good , i'm , i'm quite encouraged by that . that's , that's normal pulmonary function tests . hey dragon , show me the labs . and all of your labs here look quite good here as well so , i- i- i'm very pleased . so , right now my impression of you is that you may have had an asthma exacerbation from exercising and with the allergies , so at this point in time i'd like to go ahead and prescribe you some albuterol inhaler .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: two puffs every four hours as needed for wheezing or for shortness of breath . and , i'd like to go ahead and order a pulmonary referral for you , okay . do you have any questions about that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no questions , thanks .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , order a pulmonary referral . hey dragon , order albuterol inhaler , two puffs every four hours , as needed for shortness of breath .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then i think from your reflux standpoint , everything seems stable and your migraines , i think that we should just continue on with the imitrex , as needed . does that sound good to you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that sounds fine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i wan na see you in a couple weeks and if you have this shortness of breath again , i want you to call me right away , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . good to see you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: same here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 61-year-old male presenting with shortness of breath.
Shortness of breath
The patient experienced lightheadedness and shortness of breath after a three-hour bike ride.
The doctor asks if the patient has had any fever.
The doctor asks if the patient has chest pain.
The patient reports mild chest pain that resolved after one hour.
The doctor asks if anyone in the family is sick.
The doctor asks if the patient has a cough.
The patient reports a cough associated with a cold that resolved a week ago.
The doctor asks if the patient has nausea, vomiting, or abdominal pain.
Migraines
The patient's migraines are under control with medication and avoidance of bright lights.
The doctor asks if the patient is still taking Imitrex.
Reflux
The doctor asks if the patient is doing well on Protonix.
Cataract surgery
The patient had cataract surgery 4.5 months ago and reports good vision.
The doctor initiates a physical examination.
The patient's oxygenation is normal.
Physical exam findings include a supple neck, no JVD, regular heart rhythm, no murmurs, slight bilateral expiratory wheezing, and trace lower extremity edema.
The chest X-ray is normal with no pulmonary edema or pneumonia.
Pulmonary function tests and labs are normal; the doctor suspects an asthma exacerbation.
The doctor prescribes an albuterol inhaler, 2 puffs every 4 hours as needed.
The doctor orders a pulmonary referral.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions about the plan.
The doctor confirms the patient's reflux is stable and advises continuing Imitrex for migraines.
The doctor schedules a follow-up in two weeks and instructs the patient to call if symptoms worsen.

case_description: shortness of breath; migraines;reflux;hx cataract surgery
